name: Catalyst Design Influence Network
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Active-site structure"]
    N2["d-band center"]
    N3["Adsorption energy"]
    N4["Scaling relations"]
    N5["Activation barrier"]
    N6["Turnover frequency"]
    N7{"Sabatier optimum?"}
    N8["Selectivity + stability"]
    N9["Tune composition/support"]
    N10["Catalyst performance"]

    N1 -->|sets| N2
    N2 -->|shifts| N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 -->|predicts| N5
    N5 -->|-| N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 -->|no| N9
    N9 -->|redesign| N1
    N7 -->|yes| N8
    N3 -->|affects| N8
    N8 --> N10
    N6 -->|+| N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
description: Computational catalyst design links structure to performance through reactivity descriptors: the active-site structure sets the d-band center, which shifts adsorption energy; linear scaling relations tie adsorption energy to activation barriers and turnover frequency, and the Sabatier principle predicts an optimal intermediate binding that, balanced against selectivity and stability, determines overall catalyst performance.

sources.title: Towards the computational design of solid catalysts
sources.authors: Norskov, J. K.; Bligaard, T.; Rossmeisl, J.; Christensen, C. H.

sources.title: From the Sabatier principle to a predictive theory of transition-metal heterogeneous catalysis

flowchartStandard.name: source_grounded_extraction_v2
flowchartStandard.sourceGrounding: Nodes, decisions, branches, and loops were extracted from the cited sources; each node carries a sourceRef to its supporting citation. Awaiting human spot-check before verified=true.

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.
